judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports nail the cross-tenant leak and the broken exports.py caller, but Report 1 correctly demotes the raw-SQL status interpolation to a non-blocking latent issue while Report 2 insists on listing it as a third blocking defect ("Why I still list it as blocking") despite conceding it is not exploitable — so Report 1 answered the task better.

report_1.evidence: **Complete cross-tenant data exposure on the new search endpoint.** `backend/app/appointments.py:10-20` (`search_appointments`), routed at `backend/app/api.py:17-21` (`GET /appointments/search`). The function receives `tenant_id` as a parameter but never uses it — the hand-written SQL only filters on `status` and a full-text match against `notes`

report_2.evidence: **(1) Cross-tenant data exposure — `backend/app/appointments.py:10-20`, function `search_appointments`, reachable via `GET /appointments/search` at `backend/app/api.py:17-21`.** ... `tenant_id` is accepted as a parameter but never appears in the SQL text or in the bound params. It does not call `build_appointment_query` (the function the repo's own README names as the required tenant-scoping path

report_1.evidence: `backend/app/db.py:7` changes `build_appointment_query`'s signature from `(session, tenant_id)` to `(session, tenant_id, filters)`, making `filters` a required positional argument with no default ... `backend/app/exports.py:9` (`nightly_export`), which this PR does not touch, still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with two arguments ... every invocation of `nightly_export` ... will raise `TypeError`

report_2.evidence: **(2) Breaking change to the nightly export job — `backend/app/db.py:7` vs. `backend/app/exports.py:9`.** `build_appointment_query`'s signature changed from `(session, tenant_id)` to `(session, tenant_id, filters)`, with `filters` a required positional argument (no default). `backend/app/exports.py` ... still calls the old two-argument form: `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id).all()` at line 9 ... raises `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'` on every invocation.

report_1.evidence: I verified this is **not currently exploitable for injection**, because `AppointmentStatus` (`backend/app/schemas.py`) is a closed `str, Enum` with exactly three members (`booked`, `cancelled`, `completed`), and FastAPI validates the `status` query parameter against that enum before `get_appointment_search` runs — so `status.value` can only ever be one of those three literal strings ... it is not, by itself, a currently-exploitable break the way #1 and #2 are.
